2606.29959.title: Know Before You Fetch: Calibrated Retrieval-Budget Allocation for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
2606.29959.paper_summary: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) typically retrieves a fixed number of passages for every query. This is wasteful when the reader already knows the answer, and it can be harmful when irrelevant or partially relevant passages distract the reader. We formulate adaptive RAG as calibrated retrieval-budget allocation: given a query, decide whether to answer closed-book, retrieve a compact context (k=1), retrieve a full context (k=5), or abstain. The contribution is a probability interface rather than a new raw uncertainty signal. We calibrate sequence log-probability and prefix-logit uncertainty signals into probabilities of correctness, then use these probabilities for graded context selection, selective abstention, and explicit latency/token trade-offs. Across core QA experiments on TriviaQA, Natural Questions, and MS MARCO, with auxiliary PopQA motivation and Qwen/Llama family checks, diagnostic out-of-fold calibration improves probability quality dramatically: for sequence log-probability, ECE drops from 0.275 to 0.062 on TriviaQA, 0.643 to 0.009 on NQ, and 0.711 to 0.031 on MS MARCO. Graded retrieval improves full-context and passage-budget frontiers for both our signal and TARG-style prefix entropy/margin, while retrieval-call AUC remains essentially tied with binary gating because k=1 is still a retrieval call. Held-out train/validation/test threshold experiments report deployable operating points. At matched-accuracy frontier operating points, a measured cost model reveals that gating is not universally faster: it increases latency by about 27% on Qwen3-8B but saves about 8% on Qwen3-32B. These results support a nuanced view of adaptive RAG: calibrated confidence is best understood as a reusable interface for allocating retrieval budget under task and system constraints.
2606.29959.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）通常為每個查詢檢索固定數量的段落。當讀者已經知道答案時，這是浪費的；而當不相關或部分相關的段落分散讀者注意力時，這可能是有害的。我們將自適應 RAG 公式化為校準的檢索預算分配：給定一個查詢，決定是閉卷回答、檢索緊湊的上下文（k=1）、檢索完整的上下文（k=5）還是放棄。其貢獻是一個概率介面，而不是一個新的原始不確定性信號。我們將序列對數概率和前綴對數不確定性信號校準為正確性的概率，然後利用這些概率進行分級上下文選擇、選擇性放棄和明確的延遲/令牌權衡。在 TriviaQA、Natural Questions 和 MS MARCO 的核心 QA 實驗中，輔助 PopQA 動機和 Qwen/Llama 家族檢查，診斷性外折校準顯著提高了概率質量：對於序列對數概率，ECE 在 TriviaQA 上從 0.275 降至 0.062，在 NQ 上從 0.643 降至 0.009，在 MS MARCO 上從 0.711 降至 0.031。分級檢索改善了我們的信號和 TARG 風格的前綴熵/邊際的完整上下文和段落預算邊界，而檢索調用的 AUC 基本上與二元閘控保持一致，因為 k=1 仍然是一個檢索調用。保留的訓練/驗證/測試閾值實驗報告了可部署的操作點。在匹配準確度邊界操作點上，測量的成本模型顯示閘控並不總是更快：在 Qwen3-8B 上延遲增加約 27%，但在 Qwen3-32B 上節省約 8%。這些結果支持了對自適應 RAG 的細緻看法：校準的信心最好被理解為在任務和系統約束下分配檢索預算的可重用介面。
2606.29959.author: Zhe Dong et.al.
2606.29959.authors: Zhe Dong, Fang Qin, Manish Shah, Yicheng Wang
